_note: Showcase memo requests (§14 P5). Pg, resource triples and well cost are USER-SUPPLIED SCENARIO ASSUMPTIONS (§9.8 — no model Pg exists); each carries its rationale and is printed as such in the memo. Coordinates are open-water points chosen so the §10.4 classifier honestly yields fpso_standalone (the one concept with a complete cited cost row — see cost_benchmarks.csv gaps).

blocks._assumptions: Pg 0.22 ≈ mature-deepwater wildcat scenario; resource triple lognormal-consistent around 150 MMbbl; deepwater GoM well cost mid-range of cited benchmarks. USER-SUPPLIED.
Pg 0.25 ≈ NCS technical-success neighbourhood (label DB shows 45.6% incl. non-commercial; commercial-scale scenario set lower); Vøring deepwater FPSO case. USER-SUPPLIED.
Frontier scenario: Pg 0.15 pre-drill frontier wildcat; large lognormal-consistent resource range reflecting Orange Basin scale stories; high deepwater frontier well cost. USER-SUPPLIED.

Marginal deepwater accumulation: small resource (P50 45 MMbbl) against a high deepwater well cost — designed to land EMV>0 but P(EMV>0) below the 0.6 CONDITIONAL threshold under price/capex volatility. USER-SUPPLIED scenario.

Highly speculative frontier wildcat: Pg 0.05 against a 160 MUSD deepwater well — designed so the (1-Pg) dry-hole charge drives EMV negative → NO_GO. Demonstrates the deterministic verdict on an uneconomic prospect. USER-SUPPLIED scenario.
